items.title: Honkai: Star Rail Opens First Round of Ticket Sales for Immersive Exhibition 'Memoirs of the Trail'
items.summary: Source: HoYoverse ©HoYoverseHoYoverse Korea announced on the 8th that the first round of ticket sales for 'Memoirs of the Trail,' the first immersive exhibition for the galact...
items.content:
HoYoverse Korea announced on the 8th that the first round of ticket sales for 'Memoirs of the Trail,' the first immersive exhibition for the galactic fantasy RPG Honkai: Star Rail in Korea, is now open on Melon Ticket.

The first round of sales, which begins at 6:00 PM, covers admission for July 17 to July 31. A second round of sales will open on July 15 at 6:00 PM for tickets covering the period from August 1 to August 31.

Memoirs of the Trail' will be held from July 17 to August 31 at the immersive platform DEEP, located near the Lotte World Ice Rink (B3F) in Jamsil, Seoul. This marks the first time an immersive exhibition based on the Honkai: Star Rail IP has been held in Korea.

Spanning approximately 450 pyeong (approx. 1487 square meters), the exhibition is designed to let Trailblazers walk through and experience their three-year journey and legacy in the game. Upon entering, visitors become Trailblazers themselves, boarding the Astral Express to view records and special videos covering their travels through Jarilo-VI, the Xianzhou Luofu, Penacony, and Amphoreus, all the way to the Eternal Paradise.

The exhibition will also feature character blueprints that offer a deeper look at the companions who have shared in the journey, as well as realistic, life-sized weapon displays. Visitors can also enjoy photo zones and interactive content that make them feel as though they have stepped directly into the game.

In particular, the release of exclusive exhibition illustrations—featuring new characters Silver Wolf LV.999 and Himeko Nova visiting the Lotte World Ice Rink—has heightened fan anticipation. Additionally, a web event exclusive to the exhibition will offer in-game items, such as Stellar Jade, upon the completion of viewing missions, adding both fun and rewards.

Over 150 types of official merchandise will be available for purchase, including 20 special limited-edition items created to commemorate the exhibition and pre-orders for the 'Memoirs of the Trail' exhibition catalog. Ticket holders will receive one of nine random holographic tickets as a visitor perk and gain free access to a fan art zone showcasing illustrations created by the community.

More detailed information can be found on the official X (formerly Twitter) account for the Honkai: Star Rail immersive exhibition.

items.title: The Holy Grail War Comes to Honkai: Star Rail Once Again in the Fate/Stay Night Collaboration Event!
items.summary: The second Honkai: Star Rail x Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] collaboration event is right around the corner, and with it comes the...
items.content:
The second Honkai: Star Rail x Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] collaboration event is right around the corner, and with it comes the return of old friends and new faces. The events kick off as a magically drained Rin Tohsaka appears in Planarcadia. Using a Holy Grail displayed as part of the Star Rail FEST collection, Rin inadvertently causes the King of Heroes, Gilgamesh's, treasures to be scattered across the arcadia, leading to commotion and riots. It is up to the Trailbalzer and the young Tohsaka to set things right.

Rin herself is a 5-star Quantum-Type character following the Path of Erudition, skilled in using gems in battle. During combat, she gains a set amount of "Gem Energy" and deals multiple additional instances of DMG to a random target by consuming said power.

Gilgamesh, on the other hand, is a 5-star Lightning-Type character following the Path of Destruction. After entering battle, each ally unit taking action can accumulate "Interest" for Gilgamesh, regenerate a set amount of Energy, and increase the DMG of his Ultimate.

From the start of the collaboration on the 24th of July 24 until before Version 4.6 ends, Trailblazers can log into the game and claim Gilgamesh or Archer as one of the free limited 5-star collaboration characters, along with a portion of the corresponding upgrade materials.

GameGrin are proud to have all their articles researched, written, and edited by real people that care about gaming.

items.title: 'Honkai: Star Rail' Version 4.4 "In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound" Debuts 15 July, UGREEN Collab Details
items.summary: Late last week, publisher HoYoverse announced that Version 4.4, dubbed "In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound", will debut for its very popular free-to-play
items.content:
Late last week, publisher HoYoverse announced that Version 4.4, dubbed “In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound”, will debut for its very popular free-to-play turn-based role-playing game title ‘Honkai: Star Rail’ on 15 July.

This time around, Planarcadia’s short-term peace is shattered as the Lord Ravager Asat Pramad unveiled his disguise to reveal his true self. In this decisive battle, players will pursue leads on Himeko in an effort to find them as Asat’s obsession to uncover the fire-red lady’s true identity reverberates across the planet. Are you a bad enough dood to save Planarcadia from certain doom?! Additionally, players will continue a battle that continues through the centuries as the “Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works]” collaboration’s second phase will commence in this version. As another Holy Grail War begins, players will embark on a special chapter across the stars with Rin Tohsaka and Gilgamesh. Gilgamesh is one of the two freely claimable units until Version 4.6, joining alongside returning bow and arrow-fiend Archer.

Also unveiled late last month was accessory maker UGREEN’s collaboration with Honkai: Star Rail. Ever been told that you love Star Rail so much and that you should marry it? Well, this isn’t really the equivalent of that, but you can have the game with you thanks to the company’s latest collaboration lineup of travel and charging accessories. Themed as “Power Up for the Next Trailblaze,” this collaboration centres around the highly popular playable character Yao Guang, introducing five core pieces of on-the-go gear: a 13.9mm ultra-thin magnetic power bank, a 65W fast charger, a 10,000mAh smart display fast-charging power bank, a 100W retractable fast-charging data cable, and an anti-loss tracker that supports the Apple Find My ecosystem. More deets on all this will be text dumped after the version notes:

After the conflict broke out, Himeko vanished during her confrontation with Asat Pramad. Before she disappeared, she left behind a hidden clue related to the Express. In this version’s story, the Trailblazer tracks down and deciphers the clue to locate Himeko. Asat Pramad’s obsession with Himeko’s identity and the truth from fifteen years ago may just be the key to unraveling his motive. Ultimately, the Trailblazer will face Asat Pramad in battle. Will Planarcadia survive this game of destruction threatening its existence?

In this decisive battle, Himeko will join the fight in her new form: Himeko • Nova. The banner for the 5-star character Himeko • Nova will last throughout the entire Version 4.4. Himeko • Nova is a Fire-Type character following the Path of Erudition. When the battle begins, she immediately deploys a Territory and summons the mech “Starblazer” to assist in battle.

When using her Ultimate, she leads her teammates to pilot “Starblazer” and attack enemies. When the Territory is deployed, all teammates additionally gain the Assist Skill, and they can summon “Starblazer” at any time to attack all enemies. If a teammate is part of the Express Crew, they can also gain special effects. Additionally, when using her Technique during overworld exploration, Himeko • Nova can also pilot her mech, aim at and attack enemies within range from high above in the sky.

Planarcadia will also encounter an unexpected turn of events. After the end of the Fifth Holy Grail War, Rin Tohsaka embarks on a journey to another world and stumbles into Planarcadia as her magical energy runs out. In her desperation, she borrows the Holy Grail displayed as part of the Star Rail FEST collection showcase. She then attempts to summon a Servant and use the Holy Grail’s power to leave this world. The overflowing Wishpower in this land restores the Holy Grail, and the King of Heroes Gilgamesh’s treasures are scattered across the arcadia. A massive commotion breaks out as a result. Upon the request of the Department of Aberration Defense, the Trailblazer works with Rin Tohsaka to locate the treasures and uncover the truth behind the Imagenae: Holy Grail War. The special chapter and second phase of the Honkai: Star Rail and Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] collaboration officially begins on July 24. Please stay tuned.

As the Holy Grail War commences once more, Rin Tohsaka joins the team as a playable character. She is a 5-star Quantum-Type character following the Path of Erudition, skilled in using gems in battle. During combat, she gains a set amount of “Gem Energy” and deals multiple additional instances of DMG to a random target by consuming the “Gem Energy” she has. The more “Gem Energy” she consumes, the more instances of additional DMG she deals. When teammates consume or recover Skill Points, CRIT DMG also increases and Rin Tohsaka accumulates “Gem Energy.”

Gilgamesh will also become a playable character. He is a 5-star Lightning-Type character following the Path of Destruction. After entering battle, each ally unit taking action can accumulate “Interest” for Gilgamesh, regenerate a set amount of Energy, and increase the DMG of his Ultimate. When accumulated “Interest” reaches its threshold, Gilgamesh unlocks his Skill and unleashes Gate of Babylon to fire weapons from his treasury, dealing multiple instances of DMG to enemies. When using his Ultimate, Gilgamesh summons Enuma Elish to deal massive DMG to all enemies.

From the start of the collaboration on July 24 until before Version 4.6 ends, Trailblazers can log into the game and claim Gilgamesh or Archer as one of the free limited 5-star collaboration characters, along with a portion of the corresponding upgrade materials for the character. The characters and Light Cones for this collaboration will be available long term, and the guarantee counts are shared between the Collaboration Character Warps of both phases. The same applies for the Collaboration Light Cone Warps. Spend a total of 200 Star Rail Special Passes in Version 4.4 to select and claim one of the limited 5-star collaboration Light Cone “I Am As You Behold” or “The Hell Where Ideals Burn” after the collaboration begins.

Limited 5-star characters Sparxie, Dan Heng • Permansor Terrae, and Evernight will return in the first half of this version for a limited time. Cerydra, Anaxa, and Aventurine will return to Warp banners in the second half of this version. During this version, Sparxie’s new outfit “Candy-Coated Waves” will also be available. A new pet “Pentaflavor Emoti,” Trailblaze Fashion “Fan-Made Lygus,” and other new content will also be added in this version, bringing even more surprises to the journey.

Lastly, Version 4.4 will also offer a variety of new events and game modes. First up, players can log in to claim a 10-pull reward once the version goes live. After the version update, the collaboration event “Fate/Star Rail Night” will also unfold as the story progresses. The Trailblazer tracks down the king’s treasures with Rin Tohsaka and gradually unravels the truth behind the Imagenae: Holy Grail War. In the version event “Antigraft Brickbuster,” the Trailblazer takes on a request from Aventurine and launches an investigation into IPC corruption by stacking data bricks. Additionally, the permanent gameplay feature “Currency Wars: Zero-Sum Game” will be expanded and updated. Some system features will also be optimized. Please stay tuned.

UGREEN is dedicated to creating tech products that deliver both exceptional quality and an outstanding experience, giving users worldwide effortless control over their digital lives. Meanwhile, Honkai: Star Rail crafts a cosmic adventure across the sea of stars. The Trailblazer embarks on an interstellar journey, traversing unknown worlds with vastly different cultures. In a vast universe where myth and sci-fi intertwine, they constantly encounter surprises and explore the unknown, writing their very own adventure epic. Driven by both brands’ shared passion for technology and exploration, the UGREEN × Honkai: Star Rail collaboration series aims to equip players with their very own highly efficient, on-the-go power gear. Whether working on the move, traveling long distances, teaming up for a gaming session, or keeping multiple devices online, players can ensure every leg of their journey always remains in a fully charged, max HP state.

This collaboration marks a massive level up for UGREEN within the HoYoverse IP ecosystem. Following their Genshin Impact collaboration, UGREEN is teaming up with Honkai: Star Rail for the very first time to launch a deeply integrated charging series giftbox. This also happens to be Yao Guang’s debut in tech crossovers. By perfectly fusing Yao Guang’s knack for “strategic masterminding” and “turning woe into weal” with UGREEN’s high-performance fast-charging system, this collaboration guarantees players an energy replenishment and power management experience truly worthy of a Reignbow Arbiter-General!

items.title: Uncover a Buried Truth in the Honkai: Star Rail Version 4.4 Trailer!
items.summary: With the most recent twists in the Honkai: Star Rail story, the more than decade-long peace of Planarcadia has come...
items.content:
With the most recent twists in the Honkai: Star Rail story, the more than decade-long peace of Planarcadia has come crumbling down. The Lord Ravager, Asat Pramad, has unveiled himself, and Himeko has suddenly disappeared, leaving behind cryptic clues for the Trailblazer to follow. In the latest update, titled In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound, our heroes scramble to uncover the reason for Asat Pramad's obsession with Himeko, as well as the secret the Astral Express hides.

Luckily, the Trailblazer is not without allies, as Himeko takes the field in a new form: Himeko • Nova! A 5-star Fire-Type character following the Path of Erudition, Himeko's new kit revolves around her new mech buddy, the Starblazer. Used in both her ultimate and as a support, the powerful bot can deal massive damage to all enemies, with Express Crew gaining additional bonuses.

Honkai: Star Rail version 4.4, "In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound," will officially launch on the 15th of July.

GameGrin are proud to have all their articles researched, written, and edited by real people that care about gaming.

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail Reveals Information On New Fate/Stay Night Content
items.summary: Honkai: Star Rail has a new update on the horizon titled "In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound." It will include new content for the Fate/Stay Night collaboration.
items.content:
Last month, Hoyoverse teased us with new banners that will come out once they update the Fate/Stay Night content. A new Rin and Gilgamesh banner. Today, we finally got information about what the content will be and when the new banners will be released.

Global interactive entertainment brand HoYoverse announced today that the brand-new Version 4.4 of Honkai: Star Rail, In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound, will launch on July 15. This version will continue the story as Planarcadia faces a crisis that threatens its survival. The Trailblazer will confront the Lord Ravager Asat Pramad and engage in an epic battle. The second phase of the Honkai: Star Rail and Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] collaboration will also commence in this version. As another Holy Grail War begins, embark on a special chapter across the stars with Rin Tohsaka and Gilgamesh.

At the end of the previous chapter, the Lord Ravager Asat Pramad unveiled his disguise to reveal his true self and shattered the peace that had been sustained for fifteen years in Planarcadia. After the conflict broke out, Himeko vanished during her confrontation with Asat Pramad. Before she disappeared, she left behind a hidden clue related to the Express. In this version’s story, the Trailblazer tracks down and deciphers the clue to locate Himeko. Asat Pramad’s obsession with Himeko’s identity and the truth from fifteen years ago may just be the key to unraveling his motive. Ultimately, the Trailblazer will face Asat Pramad in battle. Will Planarcadia survive this game of destruction threatening its existence?

In this decisive battle, Himeko will join the fight in her new form: Himeko Nova. The banner for the 5 star character Himeko Nova will last throughout the entire Version 4.4. Himeko Nova is a Fire-Type character following the Path of Erudition. When the battle begins, she immediately deploys a Territory and summons the mech “Starblazer” to assist in battle. When using her Ultimate, she leads her teammates to pilot “Starblazer” and attack enemies. When the Territory is deployed, all teammates additionally gain the Assist Skill, and they can summon “Starblazer” at any time to attack all enemies. If a teammate is part of the Express Crew, they can also gain special effects. Additionally, when using her Technique during overworld exploration, Himeko Nova can also pilot her mech, aim at and attack enemies within range from high above in the sky.

Planarcadia will also encounter an unexpected turn of events. After the end of the Fifth Holy Grail War, Rin Tohsaka embarks on a journey to another world and stumbles into Planarcadia as her magical energy runs out. In her desperation, she borrows the Holy Grail displayed as part of the Star Rail FEST collection showcase. She then attempts to summon a Servant and use the Holy Grail’s power to leave this world. The overflowing Wishpower in this land restores the Holy Grail, and the King of Heroes Gilgamesh’s treasures are scattered across the arcadia. A massive commotion breaks out as a result. Upon the request of the Department of Aberration Defense, the Trailblazer works with Rin Tohsaka to locate the treasures and uncover the truth behind the Imagenae: Holy Grail War. The special chapter and second phase of the Honkai: Star Rail and Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] collaboration officially begins on July 24. Please stay tuned.

As the Holy Grail War commences once more, Rin Tohsaka joins the team as a playable character. She is a 5-star Quantum-Type character following the Path of Erudition, skilled in using gems in battle. During combat, she gains a set amount of “Gem Energy” and deals multiple additional instances of DMG to a random target by consuming the “Gem Energy” she has. The more “Gem Energy” she consumes, the more instances of additional DMG she deals. When teammates consume or recover Skill Points, CRIT DMG also increases, and Rin Tohsaka accumulates “Gem Energy.”

Gilgamesh will also become a playable character. He is a 5-star Lightning-Type character following the Path of Destruction. After entering battle, each ally unit taking action can accumulate “Interest” for Gilgamesh, regenerate a set amount of Energy, and increase the DMG of his Ultimate. When accumulated “Interest” reaches its threshold, Gilgamesh unlocks his Skill and unleashes Gate of Babylon to fire weapons from his treasury, dealing multiple instances of DMG to enemies. When using his Ultimate, Gilgamesh summons Enuma Elish to deal massive DMG to all enemies.

From the start of the collaboration on July 24 until before Version 4.6 ends, Trailblazers can log into the game and claim Gilgamesh or Archer as one of the free limited 5-star collaboration characters, along with a portion of the corresponding upgrade materials for the character. The characters and Light Cones for this collaboration will be available long term, and the guarantee counts are shared between the Collaboration Character Warps of both phases. The same applies for the Collaboration Light Cone Warps. Spend a total of 200 Star Rail Special Passes in Version 4.4 to select and claim one of the limited 5-star collaboration Light Cone “I Am As You Behold” or “The Hell Where Ideals Burn” after the collaboration begins.

Lastly, Version 4.4 will also offer a variety of new events and game modes. First up, players can log in to claim a 10-pull reward once the version goes live. After the version update, the collaboration event “Fate/Star Rail Night” will also unfold as the story progresses. The Trailblazer tracks down the king’s treasures with Rin Tohsaka and gradually unravels the truth behind the Imagenae: Holy Grail War. In the version event “Antigraft Brickbuster,” the Trailblazer takes on a request from Aventurine and launches an investigation into IPC corruption by stacking data bricks. Additionally, the permanent gameplay feature “Currency Wars: Zero-Sum Game” will be expanded and updated. Some system features will also be optimized.

Honkai: Star Rail is now available Free-To-Play on PC , iOS , Android , Epic Games Store , and PS5 . The In Ravages Does the Whistle Sound update will go live on July 15th 2026.

items.title: Hands on with the Honkai: Star Rail v4.0 experience
items.summary: This HoyoVerse train just keeps on chugging!
items.content:
The last article I wrote for Honkai: Star Rail was going over the details of the upcoming Version 4.0 release . Since then, HoYoverse generously offered up the opportunity to play Version 4.0 and experience Planarcadia early. Having spent about a dozen hours or so playing with the new characters, experiencing the story, and generally taking in the sights and sounds of Planarcadia, I’m prepared to share my thoughts on all of it.

It’s best to start off with the main attraction of Version 4.0: Planarcadia. This new planet is a fictional world come to life and boy does it leave a fantastic first impression. Full of the kind of sights and sounds you’d expect from an Asian city brimming with life. Exploration is a blast with all the alleys and little nooks to find, the platforming segments that can either help you navigate across certain parts of the city or find chests within them. There’s also a new style of puzzles that require you to rotate a 2D plane in a 3D space to “paint” certain parts of it to make them solid and let you platform with those objects. Planarcadia is so full of whimsy and still has some of the overhang of a crime family really enabling a super unique feeling planet compared to what we’ve had with previous locations such as the Xianzhou or Amphoreus.

The two new characters following the path of Elation feel great, even if the Aha Moment is a little overstimulating sometimes. Sparxie and Yao Guang fit on a team together like a glove to demolish all the bosses I’ve thrown them against. I’m eager to see where the new path goes with time, especially since we only have the two characters currently. Even as of the live public server branch, we’re unsure when Elation Trailblazer will make their debut, so keep an eye on that as versions progress beyond 4.0. You’ll need to get a hold of new Elation Light Cones as well since they obviously can’t use the other Paths’ options. Burning skill points to increase the Elation stacks until Aha decides he’s satisfied and steps in for a big attack feels really great and it has me curious what future Elation characters might do when they trigger their own Aha Moments.

Not to mention that for new players, you’ve got the opportunity to really kickstart a team of your choice with a new planet. We’re at a point in the game where there’s so many options for characters you can pick from and build around, and 4.0 introducing Elation means that as a new player, you can jump in and get a headstart on pulling for that archetype. Especially with a new Novaflare round of buffs, there’s even more options than before bringing older characters back into the limelight. The Black Swan and Sparkle buffs are so massive that people running DoT teams or any team using an action advance support such as Bronya or Sunday have another insane option. Sparkle was always good, but her Novaflare enables her to further buff your DPS units to extreme levels. Black Swan’s Novaflare was even more insane and rocketed her back up to the top for damage even as a DoT unit. Combined with the option to pick a free character including units like Sparkle, Kafka (who works great in DoT teams), and more, you really can’t go wrong in Planarcadia and the Version 4.0 patch from what I’ve gotten to experience.

Between the free characters, the great change of pace in the story from the latter half of Amphoreus (if you’ve played it, you know), the new cityscape that is quite honestly my new favorite location in the game, and all the new characters to look forward to, you really should make a return to Star Rail if you’ve been away for a minute and are curious about where we’re at. If you’re new, definitely consider giving it a shot. There’s even a bonus to let you convert trace materials for leveling character skills at a 1 to 1 ratio this time instead of a 2 for 1, so you really can kickstart your Elation team. With characters being important again like Silver Wolf, Blade, and Himeko, as well as new faces like Sparxie, Yao Guang, Ashveil, and Pearl, I think 4.0 is just the start of a real treat of a patch cycle for Honkai: Star Rail.

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail 4.0 impressions
items.content:
I'll admit, I recently burned out on Honkai: Star Rail. Upon hearing the news that 3.8 would be the lengthiest patch yet and with content more sparse than ever before, I decided to take a break for the first time since I started playing all the way back in 1.0. The entire Amphoreus saga left me feeling drained; I thoroughly enjoyed 3.0-3.4 as excellent set-up chapters, but felt lost as the plot became a labyrinth of hidden motivations, layered twists, hours of exposition, and endless proper nouns. When looking at the story as a whole, I love the characters (Aglaea and Anaxa in particular) and the rich history of the world within the Scepter, but the later patches often felt like studying for a needlessly convoluted test. I understood the gist of what was happening, but I won't lie to you and say I could describe to you in detail what Cyrene's deal is or how Evernight fits into everything.

All that's to say: I was looking forward to the Astral Express crew taking a well-earned vacation to Planarcadia, the planet of Elation. Despite two of the game's four current versions ostensibly beginning with Akivili's disciples deciding that they need a break from the backbreaking work of saving the universe, neither has resulted in our trailblazers seeing much downtime. Unlike previous excursions, it's all hands on deck: everyone gets off at this stop (save for Black Swan's incredibly hasty and unfortunate exit prior to departure). It's fun to see the entire main cast get involved, none more welcome than Himeko — up until now, the only character trait I could've told you about her was that she's terrible at making coffee. Getting some elaboration on her backstory and giving her a personality outside of "Express mom" is long overdue.

The Trailblazer and March 7th begin their Planarcadian getaway with a suitably devious greeting by none other than our old pal Sparkle. We're given a tour of the vibrant, totally-not-Japanese-inspired streets, crawling with humans living in tentative harmony with totally-not-Penaconian-memetic-entities known as imagenae. Here, we catch a glimpse of what I hope will become one of Planarcadia's core themes: gentrification and the plight of capitalism, with a side of anti-imperialism (all perpetrated by the IPC, of course). It's bold, though, and I worry that future patches will keep these criticisms in the background rather than highlight them as Planarcadia's core issues. I would love so much for little nuggets of lore to be elaborated upon, ones that suggest a more serious political history than Aha would have you believe: what did the imagenae do before the IPC forced them to integrate into society? Were they not allowed to work at all? Were they marginalized and seen as second-class citizens? Is the Department of Aberration Defense an inherently evil organization left to determine on their own which imagenae deserve their continued existence and which do not? Is Kuchiba so hot that it doesn't even matter?

This brings us to the Synwish Syndicate, a gang formerly designed to be a respite for imagenae that feel rejected elsewhere, now, due to IPC intervention, a gang that largely exists to enable monetary gain by questionable means. Here at the syndicate's headquarters, the plot quickly begins to balloon into what feels like a collection of introductions for what's to come. We have Pearl's cold and calculating grip on the planet, Himeko and her confounding family history, the potential return of the Herald of Death, the Stellaron Hunters' unknown involvement in the Phantasmoon Games, Sunday's inadvertent entry into the Games, Evanescia's whole...thing, and Yao Guang's bottomless supply of divinations. What's most shocking is that the patch still manages to pull off a fun (if bloated) self-contained story, adding layers to one of HSR's most inscrutable characters and ending things with one of the game's best low-stakes twists.

I'm no Xianzhou Arbiter-General, but I can pretty easily peer into the future and divine a prophecy for Honkai: Star Rail: each new patch will introduce and/or discuss at length a new prophecy that cryptically divulges the fate of a character and/or planet and/or simulation masquerading as a planet. It's hard to pinpoint the moment a theme becomes a crutch, but I think HSR passed that moment long ago. In this patch, we have not one, not two, but three central characters that are defined by their relationship with prophecy and prediction — Yao Guang, Pearl, and the perennial mystery that is Elio and his script. So many in-game conversations devolve into the interpretation of prophecy, and frankly, it's becoming exhausting. We went through an entire version in which each Chrysos Heir had a poetically vague predetermined fate, and before that (and in 3.8's ret-con-y confusion) we had Sparkle's ambiguous "three deaths" for Firefly.

If this were a wholesale replacement for well-executed foreshadowing, we would have a far bigger issue. It's obvious, though, that the writing team knows how to tell a story sans-prophecy, evident in Sparkle's character-driven narrative in this very patch. Sparxie being the "true" Sparkle (as much as old Sparkle is also the "true" Sparkle) is teased so perfectly; the reveal is obvious in retrospect while being difficult to predict with the given information. It's consistently groan-inducing to see this writing cheat code being used ad nauseam within a story that could function better without it. I hope I'll be proven wrong and the game will subvert the words of its murky prophets, leaving me with egg on my face come the conclusion of this leg of the Express' journey.

4.0 is a promising new beginning, and has at the very least brought me back in terms of its story. Though there is an abundance of new proper nouns to memorize and a bevy of new and returning cast members to keep track of, the patch still takes on a welcome return to simplicity and levity (save for the shocking tonal shift right at the end, RIP the Synwish Syndicate Feb. 2026 - Feb. 2026). As long as things don't get too bogged down in extensive metaphors and tiresome exposition, I'm hopeful that Planarcadia can become the most focused version of HSR we've seen so far.
